title: Drag-to-reorder characters in Admin Dashboard

requirements.text: Add a `displayOrder` field to the character data model in the backend to persist the admin-defined sort order for characters. Expose a new update-order endpoint (e.g., `reorderCharacters`) that accepts an array of character IDs in the desired order and updates their `displayOrder` values accordingly.

quotes: allow admin to be able to drag the character into the order ther desire

acceptanceCriteria: Characters have a persisted `displayOrder` field in the backend.
A `reorderCharacters` (or equivalent) function accepts an ordered list of character IDs and updates their sort order.
Characters returned by the existing `getCharacters` query are sorted by `displayOrder` ascending.
The reorder endpoint is restricted to admin principals only.

requirements.text: In the `AdminCharactersPanel` component, implement drag-and-drop reordering of the character list. Each character row/card should be draggable. When an admin drops a character into a new position, the new order should be immediately reflected in the UI (optimistic update) and persisted to the backend via the reorder mutation.

quotes: when admins are veiwing the characters in the admindashbord, allow admin to be able to drag the character into the order ther desire

acceptanceCriteria: Character rows/cards in `AdminCharactersPanel` have a visible drag handle icon.
Admin can drag a character card and drop it in a new position within the list.
The list reorders visually in real time while dragging.
On drop, the new order is sent to the backend reorder endpoint.
On successful save, the React Query cache is invalidated/updated to reflect the persisted order.
The saved order is reflected when the admin refreshes the page.
Existing edit and delete actions per character remain fully functional after reordering.
Drag-and-drop is disabled for non-admin users.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo.
Reorder endpoint must be admin-only, consistent with existing MixinAuthorization access control patterns.
nonGoals: Drag-and-drop reordering on the public-facing CharactersSection (front site).
Reordering of episodes, content, or any other entity types.
Animated transitions beyond standard drag-and-drop visual feedback.
